actors.name: Validator
actors.description: Operates a node to participate in consensus, proposes blocks, stakes $BERA, directs $BGT emissions to Reward Vaults, and earns rewards ($BGT, fees, incentives). (doc_7, doc_20, doc_24)

actors.name: User (General Blockchain User)
actors.description: Individual interacting with the Berachain ecosystem using a wallet to manage assets, sign transactions, and interact with dApps. (doc_5, doc_6, doc_18)
capabilities: Manage private keys/seed phrase via wallet
Hold and transfer tokens ($BERA, $HONEY, NFTs, etc.)
Interact with dApps (e.g., BEX, BeraHub, Honey Swap)

actors.name: Liquidity Provider (User)
actors.description: A user who provides assets to liquidity pools (e.g., on BEX) to earn trading fees and $BGT by staking LP tokens in Reward Vaults. (doc_7, doc_11, doc_25)
capabilities: Deposit assets into liquidity pools (e.g., BEX)
Stake LP tokens in Reward Vaults

actors.name: $BGT Holder (User)
actors.description: A user who has acquired $BGT, enabling participation in governance and PoL reward mechanisms. (doc_3, doc_7, doc_16, doc_27)
capabilities: Earn a share of Validator incentives and dApp fees

actors.name: Protocol / Ecosystem Project
actors.description: An entity or team that deploys dApps, including Reward Vaults, on Berachain and may offer incentive tokens to attract $BGT emissions and liquidity. (doc_14, doc_21, doc_24, doc_25)
capabilities: Request Reward Vault whitelisting via governance
Offer incentive tokens in their Reward Vaults
Manage incentive rates for their Reward Vaults

actors.name: Guardian
actors.description: A 5-of-9 multisig entity with the power to cancel malicious governance proposals during the timelock period to protect the ecosystem. (doc_3)
capabilities: Cancel malicious proposals via multisig action

actors.name: NFT Holder (User)
actors.description: A user who owns official Berachain NFTs (e.g., Bong Bears). These NFTs can be bridged and may confer benefits like airdrop eligibility. (doc_2, doc_4)
capabilities: Bridge NFTs between Ethereum and Berachain
Receive airdrops or other benefits associated with NFT ownership

actors.name: Airdrop Claimant (User)
actors.description: A user who checks their eligibility and claims $BERA tokens from an airdrop distribution. (doc_2)
capabilities: Check airdrop allocation (wallet address or social login)
Claim $BERA tokens via the airdrop application

assets.name: $BERA
assets.description: Native gas token for transactions and staking token for validators on Berachain. (doc_26, doc_29)

assets.name: $BGT (Bera Governance Token)
assets.description: Non-transferable (soulbound) token used for governance (voting, proposals) and PoL rewards (delegation to validators, earning incentives). Earned by participating in PoL. (doc_27, doc_29)

assets.name: $HONEY
assets.description: Native, fully collateralized, USD-pegged stablecoin of the Berachain ecosystem. (doc_12, doc_28)

assets.name: Berachain NFTs
assets.description: Official Non-Fungible Tokens of the Berachain ecosystem (e.g., Bong Bears, Bond Bears, Boo Bears, Baby Bears, Band Bears, Bit Bears). Can be bridged from Ethereum. (doc_4)

assets.name: LP Tokens / Receipt Tokens
assets.description: Tokens representing a user's share in a liquidity pool (e.g., from BEX) or other staked positions, used for staking in Reward Vaults to earn $BGT. (doc_7, doc_18, doc_25)

assets.name: Wallet Private Keys / Seed Phrase
assets.description: Secret cryptographic keys or mnemonic phrase that give a user control over their on-chain assets and identity. Managed by User Wallet Software. (doc_5)

assets.name: Governance Proposal Data
assets.description: Information constituting a governance proposal, including proposed changes, targets, and execution details. Managed by the Governance System. (doc_3)

assets.name: Incentive Tokens
assets.description: Whitelisted ERC20 tokens offered by Protocols within their Reward Vaults to Validators in exchange for directing $BGT emissions. (doc_15, doc_21)

assets.name: Bridged Assets
assets.description: Tokens or NFTs that have been transferred from another blockchain (e.g., Ethereum) to Berachain, or vice-versa, via a bridge. (doc_4, doc_6)

assets.name: Airdrop Allocation Data
assets.description: Data determining a user's eligibility and amount for a $BERA airdrop, checked via the Airdrop Claim Application. (doc_2)

assets.name: $HONEY Collateral
assets.description: Whitelisted assets (e.g., $USDC, $BYUSD) deposited into Honey Vaults to mint $HONEY. (doc_28)

components.name: Berachain L1 Blockchain
components.description: The core Layer 1 proof-of-liquidity blockchain network, encompassing consensus, execution (EVM), and state storage. (doc_9)

components.name: BeaconKit
components.description: The modular consensus layer framework used by Berachain, integrating CometBFT for consensus. Operates as part of the Berachain L1. (doc_8, doc_9)

components.name: EVM (Ethereum Virtual Machine)
components.description: The execution environment within Berachain L1 that runs smart contract code. (doc_9)

components.name: BEX (Berachain Exchange)
components.description: Berachain's native decentralized exchange (DEX) consisting of smart contracts for token swaps and liquidity provision. (doc_11, doc_13)

components.name: Reward Vaults
components.description: Smart contracts where users stake PoL-eligible assets (e.g., LP tokens) to receive $BGT emissions directed by validators. Protocols can offer incentives through these vaults. (doc_3, doc_7, doc_25)

components.name: BeraHub
components.description: A central dApp (frontend and smart contracts) for users to manage $BGT (boost, redeem, claim rewards), access BEX liquidity pools, and participate in Berachain governance. (doc_13, doc_15, doc_16, doc_17)

components.name: User Wallet Software
components.description: Client-side application (e.g., MetaMask, Rabby) used by users to manage private keys, sign transactions, and interact with dApps on Berachain. (doc_5)

components.name: Berachain Bridge
components.description: System (smart contracts on Berachain and other chains, possibly with off-chain components) enabling the transfer of assets like tokens (e.g., $BERA from CEX) and NFTs between Berachain and other blockchains (e.g., Ethereum). (doc_6, doc_4 for NFT bridge)

components.name: Governance System
components.description: A set of smart contracts on Berachain L1 that manage the lifecycle of governance proposals: creation, pending state, active voting, outcome determination, timelock, and execution. (doc_3)

components.name: RPC Node
components.description: A server that provides an API for wallets and dApps to read data from the Berachain blockchain and submit transactions to the network. (doc_5 - implied for wallet connection)

components.name: Airdrop Claim Application
components.description: A web application and associated smart contracts allowing eligible users to check their $BERA airdrop allocation and claim their tokens. (doc_2)

components.name: Honey Swap Application
components.description: A dApp (frontend and smart contracts, including HoneyFactory and Honey Vaults) that allows users to mint $HONEY by depositing whitelisted collateral, or redeem $HONEY for collateral. (doc_12, doc_28)

components.name: Berachain NFT Bridge Application
components.description: A dedicated web application and smart contracts for bridging official Berachain NFT collections between Ethereum and Berachain. (doc_4)

components.name: BGTIncentiveDistributor Contract
components.description: Smart contract responsible for managing the distribution and claiming of incentive tokens to users who have boosted validators with their $BGT. (doc_21)

components.name: FeeCollector Contract
components.description: Smart contract that collects fees from Berachain core dApps (BEX, Honey Swap), auctions them for $WBERA, and distributes the $WBERA to $BGT holders who have boosted validators. (doc_27)

components.name: Distributor Contract (BGT Emissions)
components.description: Smart contract that creates $BGT tokens per block (base and variable emissions) and distributes them to Reward Vaults based on validator allocations. (doc_20)

data_flows.description: Validator stakes $BERA on the Berachain L1 to participate in consensus. (doc_7, doc_20, doc_26)
Berachain L1 (BeaconKit) assigns block proposal rights to a Validator based on their $BERA stake. (doc_7, doc_20)
Validator proposes a new block to the Berachain L1 network. (doc_7)
Berachain L1 signals the Distributor Contract upon successful block production by a validator. (doc_20)
Distributor Contract sends base $BGT emission directly to the block-producing Validator. (doc_20)

data_flows.data: Reward Allocation Configuration (Weights for Reward Vaults)
data_flows.description: Validator configures their preferences for how variable $BGT emissions should be distributed across whitelisted Reward Vaults. (doc_7, doc_20)
Distributor Contract sends variable $BGT emissions to Reward Vaults chosen by the Validator, proportional to their boost and allocation weights. (doc_7, doc_20, doc_25)
Liquidity Provider deposits assets into a BEX liquidity pool via their Wallet. (doc_7, doc_11, doc_13)
BEX mints and sends LP tokens to the Liquidity Provider representing their share in the pool. (doc_7, doc_18)
Liquidity Provider stakes their LP tokens into a whitelisted Reward Vault via BeraHub or directly. (doc_7, doc_18, doc_25)
Reward Vault accrues $BGT rewards for a $BGT Holder (who staked LP tokens). User claims via BeraHub. (doc_13, doc_18, doc_25, doc_27)
$BGT Holder delegates their $BGT to a chosen Validator via BeraHub to boost the validator's emissions. (doc_7, doc_13, doc_16, doc_27)

data_flows.data: Incentive Token Deposit and Rate Configuration
data_flows.description: Protocol/Ecosystem Project deposits Incentive Tokens into their Reward Vault and sets the distribution rate per $BGT. (doc_15, doc_21)

data_flows.data: Incentive Tokens (based on $BGT emissions directed by validators)
data_flows.description: Reward Vault transfers earned Incentive Tokens to the BGTIncentiveDistributor contract for validators and their boosters. (doc_21)

data_flows.data: Validator's Commission Share of Incentive Tokens
data_flows.description: BGTIncentiveDistributor sends the validator's commission share of incentives to the Validator. (doc_21)
BGTIncentiveDistributor makes the booster's share of incentives available for claim by the $BGT Holder via BeraHub. (doc_13, doc_17, doc_21)
$BGT Holder creates or votes on a governance proposal using their $BGT via BeraHub, interacting with the Governance System. (doc_3, doc_13)
Governance System executes a successful proposal, potentially changing state on Berachain L1 or its dApps. (doc_3)
User initiates a token swap on BEX via their Wallet. (doc_11, doc_13)

data_flows.data: Asset Bridging Transaction (e.g., NFT from Ethereum)
data_flows.description: NFT Holder initiates bridging of an NFT to Berachain via the Berachain NFT Bridge Application and their Wallet. (doc_4, doc_113)

data_flows.data: Bridged Asset (e.g., Berachain representation of NFT)
data_flows.description: Berachain Bridge delivers the bridged asset to the NFT Holder's wallet on Berachain. (doc_4, doc_113)

data_flows.data: Airdrop Claim Request (Wallet Address / Social Login)
data_flows.description: Airdrop Claimant interacts with the Airdrop Claim Application to check eligibility and initiate a claim. (doc_2)
Airdrop Claim Application distributes $BERA tokens to the claimant's wallet if eligible. (doc_2)
User deposits whitelisted collateral into the Honey Swap Application to mint $HONEY. (doc_12, doc_28)
Honey Swap Application mints and sends $HONEY to the User. (doc_12, doc_28)
Guardian submits a transaction via their multisig to the Governance System to cancel a malicious proposal during its timelock. (doc_3)

data_flows.data: Private Key/Seed Phrase Input (for wallet setup/recovery)
data_flows.description: User inputs sensitive cryptographic material into their Wallet Software. This flow is local to the user's device. (doc_5)
User Wallet Software sends a signed transaction or a data read request to an RPC Node. (doc_5)

data_flows.data: Transaction for Propagation / Blockchain Data Request
data_flows.description: RPC Node relays the transaction to the Berachain L1 network or queries the L1 for data. (doc_5)
Berachain L1 provides a transaction receipt or requested data back to the RPC Node.
RPC Node forwards the transaction status or queried data to the User Wallet Software.
BEX (and other core dApps like Honey Swap) send collected fees to the FeeCollector contract. (doc_27)
FeeCollector contract distributes $WBERA (from auctioned fees) to $BGT Holders who have boosted validators. Claimed via BeraHub. (doc_27)

trust_boundaries.name: User Device Boundary
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary between the User and their local Wallet Software. Trust depends on user practices and wallet security. Validation of software authenticity is critical.

trust_boundaries.name: Wallet to dApp/RPC Boundary
trust_boundaries.description: Communication between User Wallet Software and an RPC Node (which then communicates with dApp backends/smart contracts). User trusts wallet to sign correctly; dApp/RPC interaction should be over secure channels (HTTPS/WSS). (doc_5)

trust_boundaries.name: RPC Node to Blockchain Boundary
trust_boundaries.description: RPC Node interacts with the Berachain L1. Trust in RPC node for honest relay and data provision, though data is verifiable on-chain.

trust_boundaries.name: dApp Smart Contract Boundary (e.g., BEX, Reward Vaults)
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary defining interactions with a dApp's smart contracts deployed on Berachain L1. Trust in the smart contract code's correctness and security (audits). (doc_11, doc_25)

trust_boundaries.name: Berachain L1 Consensus Boundary
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary between the Berachain L1 (specifically its consensus mechanism like BeaconKit) and the Validators. Relies on PoL economic security and honest majority of validators. (doc_7, doc_8)

trust_boundaries.name: Governance System Boundary
trust_boundaries.description: Interaction between $BGT Holders and the Governance System smart contracts. Trust in the governance contracts and process. (doc_3)

trust_boundaries.name: Berachain to External Blockchain Boundary (via Bridge)
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary for asset transfers between Berachain and other blockchains (e.g., Ethereum) facilitated by the Berachain Bridge. Trust in bridge security (contracts and any off-chain components). (doc_6, doc_4)

trust_boundaries.name: Guardian to Governance System Boundary
trust_boundaries.description: Guardians interact with the Governance System to cancel proposals. Trust in the Guardians' integrity and the multisig mechanism. (doc_3)

trust_boundaries.name: Airdrop Application Boundary
trust_boundaries.description: Users interact with the Airdrop Claim Application. Trust in the application's frontend and smart contracts to correctly distribute airdrops. (doc_2)

behaviors.name: Validator Secures Network via PoL
behaviors.action: Stake $BERA, participate in consensus, propose blocks
behaviors.description: Validator stakes $BERA, runs node software (BeaconKit), proposes valid blocks, and contributes to network security. (doc_7, doc_20, doc_26)

behaviors.name: Validator Earns Network Rewards & Incentives
behaviors.action: Receive $BGT emissions, transaction fees, and protocol incentives
behaviors.description: For participating in consensus and directing $BGT emissions, Validators earn base $BGT, transaction fees, and incentives from protocols via Reward Vaults and BGTIncentiveDistributor. (doc_7, doc_20, doc_21)

behaviors.name: User Provides Liquidity to BEX
behaviors.action: Deposit assets into liquidity pool, receive LP tokens
behaviors.description: Liquidity Provider deposits tokens into a BEX pool and receives LP tokens in return. (doc_7, doc_11)

behaviors.name: User Stakes LP Tokens to Earn $BGT
behaviors.action: Stake LP tokens in Reward Vault
behaviors.description: User (as Liquidity Provider) stakes LP tokens in a whitelisted Reward Vault to become eligible for $BGT emissions. (doc_7, doc_18, doc_25)

behaviors.name: User ($BGT Holder) Boosts Validator
behaviors.description: $BGT Holder uses BeraHub to delegate (boost) their $BGT to a Validator, increasing the validator's potential $BGT emissions and earning a share of incentives. (doc_7, doc_13, doc_16)

behaviors.name: User Participates in Governance
behaviors.action: Create proposal, vote on proposal using $BGT
behaviors.description: $BGT Holder uses BeraHub or interacts directly with Governance System contracts to create or vote on proposals affecting the Berachain ecosystem. (doc_3, doc_13, doc_27)

behaviors.name: User Swaps Tokens on BEX
behaviors.description: User interacts with BEX (likely via BeraHub or other frontend) to exchange one token for another. (doc_11, doc_13)

behaviors.name: User Bridges Assets
behaviors.description: User utilizes the Berachain Bridge (or NFT Bridge for NFTs) to transfer assets between Berachain and another compatible blockchain. (doc_4, doc_6, doc_113)

behaviors.name: User Claims $BERA Airdrop
behaviors.description: Eligible user interacts with the Airdrop Claim Application to receive their $BERA airdrop. (doc_2)

behaviors.name: User Mints/Redeems $HONEY
behaviors.action: Deposit collateral to mint $HONEY, or burn $HONEY to redeem collateral
behaviors.description: User interacts with the Honey Swap Application to mint $HONEY using approved collateral or redeem $HONEY for collateral. (doc_12, doc_28)

behaviors.name: Protocol Offers Incentives via Reward Vault
behaviors.action: Deposit incentive tokens and set rates
behaviors.description: Protocol/Ecosystem Project supplies their Reward Vault with incentive tokens to attract $BGT emissions from Validators. (doc_15, doc_21)

behaviors.name: Guardian Cancels Malicious Governance Proposal
behaviors.action: Submit cancellation transaction for a proposal in timelock
behaviors.description: Guardian multisig executes a transaction to cancel a governance proposal deemed malicious during its timelock period. (doc_3)

behaviors.name: User Manages Wallet and Signs Transactions
behaviors.action: Store private keys, approve/reject transactions, view balances
behaviors.description: User interacts with their chosen Wallet Software to manage their cryptographic keys, authorize operations, and view their assets. (doc_5)

behaviors.name: User Redeems $BGT for $BERA
behaviors.action: Initiate one-way burn of $BGT to receive $BERA
behaviors.description: $BGT Holder uses BeraHub to redeem (burn) their $BGT for an equivalent amount of $BERA. (doc_13, doc_27)
